rationale: In the citing paper, the Rao and Wang (2017) article does not play an identifiable role in the core argument about minimum-wage effects on calories and nutrition. The study's framing, identification strategy, and outcome construction are developed using other prior work (e.g., food insecurity, SNAP, nutrition-index, and DiD-method references), not this FTC false-claims paper. This indicates the citation functions as peripheral background/literature coverage rather than a meaningful building block.
